ogTitle: Lawgi Dawes vs Lawn Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lawgi Dawes and Lawn Hill.
heading: Lawgi Dawes vs Lawn Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lawgi Dawes, Queensland and Lawn Hill, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lawn Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Lawgi Dawes (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lawgi Dawes or Lawn Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lawgi Dawes has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Lawn Hill scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lawgi Dawes scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Lawn Hill scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lawgi Dawes or Lawn Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lawn Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
